questions.q: In which year was the first Partition of Bengal carried out by the British?
questions.explanation: The Partition of Bengal was carried out in 1905, taking effect in October that year.

questions.q: Which British Viceroy ordered the 1905 Partition of Bengal?
questions.explanation: Lord Curzon, then Viceroy of India, ordered the partition of the Bengal Presidency in 1905.

questions.q: The 1905 Partition of Bengal directly sparked which nationwide movement?
questions.explanation: Protests against the partition launched the Swadeshi movement to boycott British goods.

questions.q: In which year was the Partition of Bengal annulled?
questions.explanation: Facing sustained protest, the British annulled the partition in 1911.

questions.q: The 1905 partition divided which province along religious and regional lines?
questions.explanation: The province of Bengal was divided, a move widely seen as fostering Hindu-Muslim division.

questions.q: The British officially justified the 1905 partition on what grounds?
questions.explanation: The official reason given was that Bengal was too large to administer efficiently.

questions.q: Nationalists believed the real aim of the 1905 partition was to do what?
questions.explanation: Nationalists saw the partition as a deliberate attempt to divide and weaken the nationalist movement in Bengal.

questions.q: Which song became a rallying cry during the anti-partition agitation of 1905?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' became a powerful rallying cry during the Swadeshi-era anti-partition protests.

questions.q: As part of protests against the partition, people tied which thread to symbolize the unity of Bengal?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore urged Bengalis to tie rakhis to symbolize the unity and brotherhood of a divided Bengal.

questions.q: The Partition of Bengal mainly encouraged Indians to boycott goods of which origin?
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi response involved boycotting British goods in favour of indigenous products.

questions.q: On which date in 1905 did the Partition of Bengal formally take effect?
questions.explanation: The partition came into force on 16 October 1905, observed by nationalists as a day of mourning.

questions.q: The 1905 partition created a new province with which capital for eastern Bengal?
questions.explanation: The new province of Eastern Bengal and Assam had its capital at Dacca (Dhaka).

questions.q: What was the name of the new province carved out by the 1905 partition?
questions.explanation: The partition created the province of Eastern Bengal and Assam, separate from western Bengal.

questions.q: How did nationalists mark 16 October 1905, the day the partition took effect?
options: As a day of mourning with fasting
questions.explanation: Nationalists observed the day with fasting, hartals, and the tying of rakhis as a day of mourning.

questions.q: Which prominent Bengali leader was a key moderate voice in the anti-partition agitation?
questions.explanation: Surendranath Banerjee was a leading moderate figure in organizing protest against the partition.

questions.q: When the partition was annulled in 1911, the British also announced what other major change?
options: Shifting the capital from Calcutta to Delhi
questions.explanation: Along with annulment, the British announced the transfer of the imperial capital from Calcutta to Delhi in 1911.

questions.q: Which British monarch's 1911 Delhi Durbar coincided with the announcement annulling the partition?
questions.explanation: The annulment and capital transfer were announced at the 1911 Delhi Durbar of King George V.

questions.q: The 1905 partition is often said to have benefited the political mobilization of which community in eastern Bengal?
questions.explanation: Eastern Bengal had a Muslim majority, and the partition was seen as encouraging separate Muslim political mobilization.

questions.q: The strong reaction to the 1905 partition contributed to the founding of which organization in 1906?
questions.explanation: The political climate after the partition contributed to the founding of the All-India Muslim League at Dacca in 1906.

questions.q: Rabindranath Tagore responded to the partition by composing songs and promoting which festival of unity?
questions.explanation: Tagore promoted a Rakhi Bandhan day so Hindus and Muslims would tie rakhis as a symbol of unity.

questions.q: Curzon's partition split Bengal partly to separate Bengali-speakers from which other region joined to the east?
questions.explanation: Assam was joined with eastern Bengal to form the new province, splitting the Bengali-speaking heartland.

questions.q: The anti-partition movement strongly promoted which educational alternative to government schools?
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi response included founding national schools and colleges as alternatives to government institutions.

questions.q: Curzon's partition plan was preceded by a 1903 proposal floated by which official?
questions.explanation: Home Secretary Herbert Hope Risley's 1903 note advanced the administrative case for partitioning Bengal.

questions.q: Western Bengal, after the 1905 partition, retained its capital at which city?
questions.explanation: The reduced western province of Bengal retained Calcutta as its capital after the 1905 division.

questions.q: Which Muslim leader of eastern Bengal supported the 1905 partition, seeing advantage for his community?
questions.explanation: Nawab Salimullah of Dacca was a notable supporter of the partition and a patron of the new Muslim League.

questions.q: When Bengal was reunited in 1911, which two provinces were separated out as a distinct administration?
questions.explanation: The 1911 reorganization reunited Bengali-speaking areas and split off Bihar and Orissa as a separate province.

questions.q: The anti-partition agitation gave momentum to which revolutionary secret society active in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The unrest over the partition energized Bengal revolutionary groups such as the Anushilan Samiti.

questions.q: At which 1906 Congress session, presided over by Dadabhai Naoroji, was 'Swaraj' adopted as a goal amid the anti-partition stir?
questions.explanation: At the 1906 Calcutta session, presided over by Naoroji, the Congress adopted Swaraj as its goal during the partition agitation.

questions.q: Eastern Bengal and Assam, created in 1905, had its lieutenant-governor seated at which city?
questions.explanation: Dacca served as the capital of the new Eastern Bengal and Assam province, seat of its lieutenant-governor.

questions.q: Curzon resigned as Viceroy in 1905 over a dispute with which British military commander-in-chief in India?
questions.explanation: Curzon resigned in 1905 after a clash with Commander-in-Chief Lord Kitchener over military control, the same year as the partition.
